compositional_truth_check.p1_yellow_pot: Systemic Loss: No visible String of Nickels foliage.
compositional_truth_check.p2_black_pot: Systemic Loss: No visible Mexican Mint foliage.
compositional_truth_check.p3_black_pot: Present: Pothos specimen with white rabbit anchor.
compositional_truth_check.p4_black_pot: Systemic Loss: No visible Silver Guest foliage.

inventory_reconciliation.notes: The biome is largely devoid of the expected botanical specimens. Only the Pothos (P3) retains a single viable leaf.

plant_audit.p1: Empty soil, no signs of life.
plant_audit.p2: Empty soil, no signs of life.
plant_audit.p3: One single leaf remains; appears turgid but isolated.
plant_audit.p4: Empty soil, no signs of life.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dry and compacted; no fungal growth detected.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in P3/P4 area; electronic component (IC/header) observed on desk surface near pots.
biome_observations.surface: Desk surface is clean but lacks biological activity.

temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Return to standard lighting; Pothos leaf remains in identical position.

visual_health_inference.p3: Stagnant/Survival mode. The single leaf is the only remaining photosynthetic surface.

anomalies: Presence of non-botanical electronic component (IC header) on desk.
Eggshell fragments used as soil amendment or decorative mulch.
Total loss of 3 out of 4 registered species.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome has suffered a near-total collapse. The Pothos (P3) is the sole survivor, though it is severely diminished. The other pots (P1, P2, P4) contain only soil, indicating a systemic failure of the botanical collection. The presence of eggshells suggests an attempt at nutrient intervention, which has not prevented the loss of the other specimens.
